title: Stronger Families through Art Therapy
text:
Stronger Families through Art Therapy - SupportGoWhere
Stronger Families through Art Therapy
Share link
Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) provides art therapy interventions for children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families to build family resilience through psychoeducational workshops, group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based, Helpline, Home-based, House-call, Online
Free
Referral required
HELPLINE
1800 2744 788
Mon - Fri: 2.30pm - 5.00pm
Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

SFAT comprises a 10-session intervention, which includes 2 workshops and 8 group art therapy sessions, and an art-based self-care tool that participants can use independently with their families.      The free programme is delivered by credentialed art therapists from The Red Pencil (Singapore), in partnership with Glyph Community Ltd and Yishun Family Service Centre.

Who is this for?

The Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) programme serves children aged 7-14 and their caregivers from low-income vulnerable families. Eligible families include those receiving CHAS Blue card, ComCare financial assistance, or MOE Financial Assistance Scheme.     The programme particularly supports families experiencing caregiver stress related to finances, work-life balance, and childcare arrangements, caregivers who struggle to find time for self-care and quality time with children, those with minimal understanding of their children's emotional needs, and children facing difficulties with emotional regulation.      Families can participate as a dyad of one caregiver and one child, or a caregiver may join with up to three children aged 7-14.

What to expect?

After hearing about the programme due to publicity from Glyph Community Ltd or Yishun Family Service Centre and signing up, you will first attend a pre-session where you'll be briefed about the programme, have the opportunity to ask questions, sign consent forms, and learn whether you can start immediately or join the next available programme cycle. If confirmed, you'll participate in a 10-session programme.     The programme runs for about 3 months. The programme takes place in small group settings with up to 6 families and consists of three main components: a pre-workshop introducing art therapy (1 session), group art therapy sessions (8 sessions), and a post-workshop with introduction to self-care tool (1 session).     After completing the programme, you will receive an Art-Based Self-Care Tool for independent use at home, and your family can continue receiving support from the referring organisation if needed. You will also be asked to provide feedback about the programme through evaluation forms.

How to apply?

Interested families can apply for the Stronger Families through Art Therapy (SFAT) programme through either Glyph Community Ltd or Yishun Family Service Centre under Singapore Children's Society. These partner organisations will screen potential participants for eligibility and assess needs through their existing processes before referring eligible families to the programme.

Information last updated on 26 Sep 2025
